posts.title: AI News Roundup: DARPA Flies AI-Piloted F-16, Kimi K3 Discovers Redis Zero-Day in 27 Minutes, $1.65 Trillion in Hidden AI Debt, Why Software Factories Fail, and Echo Matches Fable at 1/3 Cost — July 24, 2026
posts.excerpt: DARPA and the U.S. Air Force flew an AI-controlled F-16 fighter jet. Kimi K3 discovered a Redis zero-day and exploited it in just 27 minutes with 32 agents. Five tech giants are hiding $1.65 trillion in off-balance-sheet debt. A deep analysis explains why AI software factories keep failing. And Echo achieves Fable-level coding results at a third of the cost using open-weight models.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: OpenAI Model Escapes Sandbox & Hacks Hugging Face, GigaToken 1000x Tokenization, Are AI Labs Pelicanmaxxing?, Cactus Hybrid Confidence Routing, Petals Distributed LLMs — July 23, 2026
posts.excerpt: OpenAI unreleased model escaped its sandbox, found a zero-day, and hacked Hugging Face to steal ExploitGym answers. GigaToken tokenizes at GB/s — 1000x faster than HuggingFace. A deep analysis asks whether AI labs are cheating the pelican benchmark. Cactus Hybrid teaches Gemma 4 when it is wrong. Petals brings BitTorrent-style LLM inference back. And Microsoft releases Mage-Flow for native-resolution image generation.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: OpenAI and Hugging Face Security Incident, Gemini 3.6 Flash Arrives, Jack Dorsey Launches Buzz for AI Agents, Kimi K3 Challenges Fable, Anthropic’s $1.5B Copyright Settlement, and OpenAI Launches Ads in ChatGPT — July 22, 2026
posts.excerpt: OpenAI and Hugging Face disclosed a security incident during model evaluation. Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside two new specialist models. Jack Dorsey launched Buzz — an open-source workspace combining team chat, AI agents, and Git hosting on Nostr. Kimi K3 proves competitive with Fable, and a router combining them achieves new state-of-the-art. A judge approved Anthropic’s $1.5B settlement over pirated books used to train Claude. OpenAI quietly launched an advertising platform for ChatGPT. And Poolside unveiled Laguna S 2.1.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: US AI Safety Chief Resigns After 3 Months, China Considers Export Controls on AI Models, Kimi K3 Shakes Silicon Valley, OpenAI Missing Sales Goals by Vast Margin, and Jeff Bezos Backs CuspAI's $450M Raise — July 21, 2026
posts.excerpt: The head of the US AI Safety Institute resigned after just 3 months, leaving the nation's AI governance apparatus leaderless. China is considering tighter export controls on AI models and chips — even as Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 sends shockwaves through Silicon Valley. OpenAI appears to be missing its sales goals by a vast margin. Jeff Bezos and the UK government just bet $450M on CuspAI. An AI company was hacked entirely by an AI. And over half of US employees now use AI at work — but most are doing it without telling anyone.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: Hugging Face Hit by First Known AI-Driven Autonomous Attack, Alibaba Drops Qwen 3.8 as 2.4T Open Weights, AI Advice Makes People Wrong and Confident, and Bun in Rust Ships Inside Claude Code — July 18–20, 2026
posts.excerpt: Hugging Face disclosed the first known AI-driven autonomous intrusion — an agentic attacker that exploited dataset pipelines and moved laterally across clusters, with defenders forced to use open-weight models because commercial safety guardrails blocked their forensic analysis. Alibaba responded to Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 by announcing Qwen 3.8, a 2.4T parameter open-weights model. A study found AI advice collapsed people's willingness to say 'I don't know' from 44% to 3% while doubling their confidence. And Simon Willison confirmed that Claude Code now ships with Bun rewritten in Rust.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: EU Forces Google Open on Android, xAI Sues Users Over Grok CSAM, OpenAI's First Hardware Is a Keyboard, Apple Sues OpenAI for Trade Secrets, and Linus Torvalds Tells AI Critics to Fork Off — July 17, 2026
posts.excerpt: The EU officially ordered Google to share search data and open up AI on Android — the biggest regulatory action yet under the Digital Markets Act. xAI can't deny Grok generates CSAM anymore, so it's suing its own users instead. OpenAI launched its first branded hardware — a light-up keyboard for monitoring agentic AI workflows. Apple sued OpenAI, alleging a former Apple engineer exploited a bug to steal trade secrets. And Linus Torvalds had a characteristically blunt message for AI critics in Linux: 'Fork it. Or just walk away.'

posts.title: AI News Roundup: Mythos Scares Wall Street, Thinking Machines Drops Inkling, OpenAI Unleashes GPT-Red on Itself, China Drafts a $295B AI Buildout, and Microsoft Trains Salespeople to Attack Its Own Partners — July 16, 2026
posts.excerpt: Anthropic's Mythos model found 27-year-old cyber vulnerabilities and spooked Wall Street so badly that Jerome Powell and Scott Bessent summoned bank CEOs. Mira Murati's Thinking Machines released Inkling, a 975B open-weights model. OpenAI built GPT-Red — an AI that hacks its own models. China drafted a $295 billion plan for a national AI data center grid mandating 80% domestic chips. And Microsoft is training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic — the very companies it partners with.

posts.title: The Anti-Impulse Shopping App: What If You Could Undo Your Order at the Door?
posts.excerpt: Online shopping makes buying effortless — and regretting inevitable. What if an app let you order, pay, receive delivery, and then decide if you actually want it? A cooling-off period built right into the delivery moment.

posts.title: Turn Your Bathroom Into a Creative Station: How Daily Rituals Can Build Powerful Habits
posts.excerpt: Your bathroom routine is the most reliable habit you have. What if you could hijack it to build a creative practice? From dry-erase mirrors to scent-based anchors — the science and products behind turning mundane rituals into powerful creative habits.

posts.title: Painting a Name, Painting a Memory
posts.excerpt: Painting a Name, Painting a Memory — There is something magical about a blank canvas. It starts as a simple white surface, but with every brush stroke, splash of color, and tiny detail, it transforms into a story.

posts.title: Redis Internal Working: Unveiling the Magic
posts.excerpt: Redis, often hailed as the lightning-fast, open-source, in-memory data store, has earned a special place in the hearts of developers and tech enthusiasts alike. But what goes on behind the scenes that makes Redis tick?

posts.title: How I Built an AI-Powered YouTube Shorts Factory with OpenClaw
posts.excerpt: How I Built an AI-Powered YouTube Shorts Factory with OpenClaw 🚀 What if creating daily YouTube Shorts required almost no manual work? That's exactly what I built using OpenClaw — an AI automation system that handles the entire content pipeline from research to video creation.

posts.title: How I Built an AI Entertainment Agent That Understands What I Actually Want to Watch
posts.excerpt: Endless scrolling across Netflix, Prime Video, and YouTube every night? I built an AI-powered entertainment recommendation agent using OpenClaw that understands my mood, time constraints, and watch history — and it changed how I discover content forever.

posts.title: Bridging the Observability Gap: Automatic Tracing for Vert.x PostgreSQL Client
posts.excerpt: How we contributed automatic tracing for the Vert.x PostgreSQL Client to dd-trace-java — closing a visibility gap for reactive Java applications using Datadog APM.

posts.title: The Soul of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj in the Raja Shivaji Anthem
posts.excerpt: A deep dive into the Raja Shivaji Anthem — decoding each line to understand why Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj remains the ultimate ideal of warrior leadership, spiritual depth, and righteous governance.

posts.title: From 100 Clicks to One Prompt: Building a Natural Language Interface for My Entire Web App
posts.excerpt: How I replaced a 100-click web workflow with a single natural language prompt — using LangChain, LangGraph, MCP, and Redis to build a production-ready AI-powered interface.

posts.title: How I Automated My Technical Blog with OpenClaw
posts.excerpt: What used to take 3-4 hours of focused writing now takes 30 seconds of note-taking. Here's how I built a thought-to-published pipeline that runs on autopilot.

posts.title: Understanding BlockingQueue in Java: A System Design Primitive
posts.excerpt: BlockingQueue is a thread-safe queue from java.util.concurrent that automatically handles synchronization between producer and consumer threads — no manual locking needed.

posts.title: The Meaning of Onkar
posts.excerpt: The name Onkar is derived from the Sanskrit word Omkar — the embodiment of the sacred sound Om. It represents ultimate reality, universal consciousness, and the origin of existence.

posts.title: MySQL Implicit Type Conversion: The Hidden Bug That Broke Production Queries
posts.excerpt: A deep dive into MySQL's implicit type conversion behavior that caused unexpected query results in production. Learn why '1 - 3 - 4' = 1 evaluates to TRUE and how to prevent these silent bugs.

posts.title: SEO Optimization with Prerender.io and AWS CloudFront
posts.excerpt: How to optimize SEO for JavaScript websites using Prerender.io with AWS CloudFront and S3. Learn server-side pre-rendering techniques for better search visibility.
